message:
test(robot_types): conformance proptests for two uncovered serde paths

Adds rt88 and rt89 to proptest_robot_types.rs closing two real conformance
gaps in the existing robot response-envelope serde coverage:

- rt88_get_text_data_with_truncation_info_serde
  The existing prop_get_text_data_serde (line 566) relies on
  arb_get_text_data (line 175), which hardcodes truncation_info: None
  and never asserts on that field. The Option<TruncationInfo> branch of
  GetTextData's serde has no regression coverage. rt88 fuzzes
  truncation_info as Some with random TruncationInfo, asserts idempotent
  JSON (serialize -> deserialize -> serialize equality), and verifies
  every TruncationInfo subfield survives the roundtrip.

- rt89_batch_get_text_data_with_results_serde
  The existing rt67_batch_get_text_data_serde (line 1942) uses an empty
  results BTreeMap, so the tagged-enum PaneTextResult inside the map is
  untested for BatchGetTextData. rt89 populates results with both
  PaneTextResult::Ok and PaneTextResult::Error entries and asserts the
  outer JSON roundtrips idempotently, both variants survive with their
  payloads intact, and the `status` tag discriminator appears.

Both tests use the "serialize, deserialize, re-serialize, compare JSON"
pattern already established by rt63/rt64 because the response structs
do not derive PartialEq; idempotent re-serialization is a strictly
stronger signal than field-by-field assertions for nested serde paths.

cargo check -p frankenterm-core --tests --offline on /tmp/ft-pane1-target
passes clean in 4m11s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
